commit.name: Weather Card
commit.description: Data display with props, design tokens, and responsive layout
commit.prompt:
Build a weather card component. It should display:
- Current temperature (large, prominent)
- Weather condition with an appropriate icon/emoji (sunny, cloudy, rainy, etc.)
- Humidity percentage
- Wind speed
- A 5-day forecast strip at the bottom showing day name, icon, and high/low temps

All data must come from props — never hardcode weather data. Use CSS variables from the design system for all colors and spacing.

issues.description: The forecast section lacks responsive behavior for the 5-day grid and feels cramped on small screens.
issues.fix: Use a grid or wrap-enabled stack for the forecast strip to ensure better spacing on mobile devices.

issues.description: The card has no visual feedback on hover, despite having a transition defined.
issues.fix: Add a :hover pseudo-class to increase the shadow depth or slightly scale the element.

issues.description: The forecast icons (emojis) are rendered without proper alt text or aria-labels.
issues.fix: Wrap icons in a span with aria-label describing the weather state (e.g., 'Sunny', 'Rainy') for screen readers.

evaluations.critique: The component is clean, well-structured, and adheres strictly to the provided API. It effectively utilizes the design primitives, though it could benefit from improved accessibility for the icons and a more robust layout for the forecast strip.

evaluation.critique: The component demonstrates strong adherence to design tokens and proper data handling from props, with good hierarchy and spacing. However, it lacks interactive polish (no hover states, focus indicators, or transitions on forecast items), and the forecast strip could benefit from better responsiveness handling for smaller screens where 5 items may cause overflow.

commit.name: Interactive Periodic Table
commit.description: CSS Grid periodic table with category coloring, search, and detail panel
commit.prompt:
Build an interactive periodic table of elements.

Layout:
- CSS Grid: 18 columns. Place each element using gridRow and gridColumn from its data.
- Each cell: symbol (large, centered), atomic number (small, top-left)
- Color-code by category using design tokens (e.g., noble-gas → one color, halogen → another)
- Search input at top — filters by name or symbol (case-insensitive)
- Click an element → detail panel below grid shows: name, symbol, atomicNumber, atomicWeight, category
- Legend showing each category with its color

All element data comes from props.elements. Use design system CSS variables.

issues.description: Legend does not apply category colors; it only displays the category names as badges.
issues.fix: Map through categories and apply the getCategoryColor logic to the Badge component background or a separate indicator.

issues.description: Cells do not reflect the category color-coding defined in the helper function.
issues.fix: Apply the getCategoryColor return value to the 'backgroundColor' style of the Card component for each element.

issues.description: Elements lose visibility when filtered out, but there is no visual feedback for elements that don't match the search; currently, the grid layout breaks.
issues.fix: Set a transparent background or low opacity for non-matching elements instead of removing them from the grid to maintain the periodic structure, or use absolute positioning/visibility hidden.

issues.description: Interactive elements are divs with onClick handlers without role='button' or keyboard accessibility (onKeyDown).
issues.fix: Add role='button', tabIndex={0}, and an onKeyDown handler to trigger selection via Enter/Space.

evaluations.critique: The component structure is clean and the grid layout approach is correct. It successfully implements the core requirements, but it fails to apply the promised color coding to the cells and lacks necessary accessibility patterns for interactive elements.

evaluation.critique: The component has significant layout issues: the 18-column grid doesn't properly position elements using row/col data (grid positioning won't work with filtered results), the overflowX workaround breaks responsive design, and cells lack proper sizing/color application. Design token usage is inconsistent—getCategoryColor returns raw var() strings instead of being applied to elements, and spacing uses hardcoded pixel values (4px, 8px, 12px) instead of design tokens.
